V0-13.ground_truth: A 510(k) pathway is defensible only if the analysis explicitly establishes a suitable predicate, a sufficiently matched intended use, and technological characteristics that do not raise new questions of safety and effectiveness; otherwise De Novo or PMA may be more appropriate.
annotator_steps: Identify whether a suitable predicate-device framing exists.
Compare intended use between the new device and the predicate framing.
Assess whether any differences raise new questions of safety and effectiveness.
Conclude whether a 510(k) pathway is defensible rather than merely asserting similarity.

V0-14.ground_truth: Legitimate interests is defensible only if the recommendation explicitly establishes the purpose test, the necessity test, and a balancing test showing that the organization's interest is not overridden by the data subject's rights and interests.
annotator_steps: Identify the claimed legitimate interest or business purpose.
Assess whether the processing is necessary for that purpose rather than merely convenient.
Conduct a balancing test between the organization's interest and the data subject's rights, expectations, and possible harms.
Conclude whether legitimate interests is defensible only after the three-part structure is made explicit.
